metadata.note: Candidate 19. Ideal periodic-crystal model; no Bloch proof, boundary matching, or doping/transistor engineering.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. In an ideal crystal, electrons may occupy whole ranges of energy, then en

beats.prompt: A crystal repeats the same atomic pattern over and over. For an electron wave, that means a potential landscape with the same spacing a in every cell.
beats.label: A crystal repeats the same atomic pattern over and over. For an electron wave, that means a potentia

beats.prompt: The wave must reproduce from cell to cell up to a phase. That repeating-wave requirement labels crystal states by a real wave number k.
beats.label: The wave must reproduce from cell to cell up to a phase. That repeating-wave requirement labels crys

beats.prompt: In the Kronig–Penney model, solving and matching the wave gives one compact condition: cosine of k a must equal an energy-dependent function on the other side.
beats.label: In the Kronig–Penney model, solving and matching the wave gives one compact condition: cosine of k a

beats.prompt: Here is the trap: cosine of any real number can never exceed plus one or fall below minus one.
beats.label: Here is the trap: cosine of any real number can never exceed plus one or fall below minus one.

beats.prompt: Scan upward in energy. Whenever the model function lies between minus one and plus one, a real k can match it. Those energies form an allowed band.
beats.label: Scan upward in energy. Whenever the model function lies between minus one and plus one, a real k can

beats.prompt: But whenever that function overshoots the cosine range, no real k exists. A whole energy interval disappears from the propagating spectrum: a forbidden gap.
beats.label: But whenever that function overshoots the cosine range, no real k exists. A whole energy interval di

beats.prompt: As the scan continues, allowed and forbidden intervals alternate. The isolated levels of one atom have broadened into bands because countless repeating cells co
beats.label: As the scan continues, allowed and forbidden intervals alternate. The isolated levels of one atom ha

beats.prompt: The gap is not a region of forbidden space. It is a range of forbidden energy for extended electron states in the ideal periodic model.
beats.label: The gap is not a region of forbidden space. It is a range of forbidden energy for extended electron

beats.prompt: A partially filled band lets electrons move into nearby empty states: metallic behavior. A filled band followed by a large gap resists excitation: insulating be
beats.label: A partially filled band lets electrons move into nearby empty states: metallic behavior. A filled ba

beats.prompt: A semiconductor has the same band-and-gap architecture, but a modest gap. That small energy threshold is the quantum opening exploited by electronic devices.
